finding: _get_rss_darwin() uses resource.getrusage(RUSAGE_SELF).ru_maxrss which returns PEAK RSS not current RSS. Metrics are incorrect on macOS.
evidence: capture/resources.py:46-52 — ru_maxrss is documented as maximum resident set size ever reached.
proposed_action: Use ctypes mach_task_info or ps -o rss= for current RSS; reserve ru_maxrss only for peak_rss_bytes field.

finding: _poll_cpu() formula double-counts children CPU time. Adds current children and subtracts previous children separately from user/system delta instead of computing (total_current - total_previous).
evidence: capture/resources.py:96-97 — formula computes user_delta + children_delta separately with partial subtraction.
proposed_action: Fix to: total_delta = (cur.user + cur.system + cur.children_user + cur.children_system) - (prev.user + prev.system + prev.children_user + prev.children_system).

finding: Proxy only implements read methods (read/readline/readlines/__next__). write() calls go through __getattr__ delegation without hash tracking so write-mode provenance hash may be inaccurate.
evidence: core.py:427 ff — no write() or writelines() method on ProvenanceFileProxy.
proposed_action: Add write()/writelines() methods that update the incremental hash for write-mode files.

finding: start() reads get_current_run() under lock but creates Run() outside the lock. Two concurrent threads can both see active=None and both create Run objects; second overwrites _active_run silently.
evidence: core.py:97-105 — lock released before Run() construction.
proposed_action: Hold _run_lock across Run() construction or use double-checked locking with re-check.

finding: If json.dumps(record) raises (non-serializable payload) the event is silently dropped with only a debug log. User gets no feedback that their annotation was lost.
evidence: events.py:82-104 — TypeError/ValueError from json.dumps caught by outer except.
proposed_action: Catch serialization errors specifically; log at warning level with event type info.

finding: If hardware thread completes after startup manifest write but process is killed before stop() the on-disk manifest has stale capture_state:pending for hardware forever.
evidence: tracker.py:159-164 vs :274-280 — startup manifest written before hardware thread finishes.
proposed_action: Re-write manifest after hardware thread completes (supplement write).

finding: end_rss_bytes calls _poll_rss() on macOS which returns ru_maxrss (peak) making end_rss always equal to peak_rss — the field is meaningless on macOS.
evidence: capture/resources.py:138-141 — same root cause as BUG-01.
proposed_action: Same fix as BUG-01: use current RSS API for polling; ru_maxrss only for peak.

finding: For write-mode files the proxy hashes from disk after close(). Relies on close() flushing buffers. Correct for standard Python files but undocumented assumption.
evidence: core.py:468-472 — close() then hash; no explicit flush before hash.
proposed_action: Document the assumption in docstring. No code change needed.

finding: SubprocessSpy._records is class-level. Records from prior install cycle can leak into new manifest if install/uninstall lifecycle is not followed perfectly.
proposed_action: Reset records in uninstall() to prevent cross-run leakage.

finding: After migrate_directory() if reopen fails emit('warning') silently does nothing (self._file is None). The migration failure warning itself is lost.
evidence: tracker.py:381-385 — emit after migration may silently fail.
proposed_action: Check stream state before emit; fall back to logger.warning().
